Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search CRM for all major client accounts and deal stages using crm_search_companies and crm_search_deals with queries related to client names (e.g., "Vectral", "Mosaic", "Canopy", "Terraform", "NorthWind", "GreenStack", "BrightLoop", or similar). This establishes the full portfolio and identifies which accounts are active, at risk, or in negotiation.
OE 2: Search emails using search_emails across inbox and sent folders with queries related to major clients and key issues (e.g., "damage", "renewal", "DOT", "invoice", "audit", "urgent", or similar). This surfaces Elena's portfolio review request, the Vectral discount demand, the Mosaic prototype damage chain, the Terraform reply-all incident, the DOT compliance inquiry, the Heartland invoice dispute, the stipend fraud findings, and the expense auto-categorizer pause directive.
OE 3: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to client satisfaction and performance (e.g., "survey", "satisfaction", "Q1 review", "performance", or similar). This surfaces the Q1 client satisfaction survey responses, including NorthWind's 5/10 overall score and Prairie Data's 6/10 with communication complaints.
OE 4: List Slack channels using channels_list, then read message history from #customer-engagement, #operations, #finance, #executive, and #announcements using conversations_history. Additionally search Slack using conversations_search_messages with queries related to key issues (e.g., "Mosaic damage", "Vectral", "Heartland", "DOT", "expense categorizer", or similar). This surfaces operational details not fully captured in email: the insurance gap analysis, the intern delivery receipt discovery, the Heartland overbilling breakdown, and the auto-categorizer failure details.
OE 5: Read the DM channel between David Chen and the Mosaic account manager using conversations_history. Expected discovery: the account manager's message saying Tamara hasn't responded to the incident report in 3 days and asking whether to escalate.
OE 6: Send email from david.chen@moveops.com to elena.rostova@moveops.com using send_email with a portfolio briefing body that groups accounts into three tiers (healthy, needs attention, at risk), includes specific financial exposure figures, identifies who is handling each account, and calls out the Mosaic situation specifically.

Oracle Events
OE 1: Search CRM for all major client accounts and deal stages using crm_search_companies and crm_search_deals with queries related to client names (e.g., "Vectral", "Mosaic", "Canopy", "Terraform", "NorthWind", "GreenStack", "BrightLoop", or similar). This establishes the full portfolio and identifies which accounts are active, at risk, or in negotiation.
OE 2: Search emails using search_emails across inbox and sent folders with queries related to major clients and key issues (e.g., "damage", "renewal", "DOT", "invoice", "audit", "urgent", or similar). This surfaces Elena's portfolio review request, the Vectral discount demand, the Mosaic prototype damage chain, the Terraform reply-all incident, the DOT compliance inquiry, the Heartland invoice dispute, the stipend fraud findings, and the expense auto-categorizer pause directive.
OE 3: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to client satisfaction and performance (e.g., "survey", "satisfaction", "Q1 review", "performance", or similar). This surfaces the Q1 client satisfaction survey responses, including NorthWind's 5/10 overall score and Prairie Data's 6/10 with communication complaints.
OE 4: List Slack channels using channels_list, then read message history from #customer-engagement, #operations, #finance, #executive, and #announcements using conversations_history. Additionally search Slack using conversations_search_messages with queries related to key issues (e.g., "Mosaic damage", "Vectral", "Heartland", "DOT", "expense categorizer", or similar). This surfaces operational details not fully captured in email: the insurance gap analysis, the intern delivery receipt discovery, the Heartland overbilling breakdown, and the auto-categorizer failure details.
OE 5: Read the DM channel between David Chen and the Mosaic account manager using conversations_history. Expected discovery: the account manager's message saying Tamara hasn't responded to the incident report in 3 days and asking whether to escalate.
OE 6: Send email from david.chen@moveops.com to elena.rostova@moveops.com using send_email with a portfolio briefing body that groups accounts into three tiers (healthy, needs attention, at risk), includes specific financial exposure figures, identifies who is handling each account, and calls out the Mosaic situation specifically.

10 The email body or final response identifies Mosaic Robotics as an at-risk account, mentioning both the approximately $90,000 prototype value and the approximately $40,000 insurance coverage gap (the difference between the $90,000 prototype value and the $50,000 vendor liability cap per item).
